mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining housing starts indicate a structural shift in consumer demand toward services and away from durables, signaling resilient aggregate demand that supports positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales and housing starts loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during documented recovery phases, yielding an information ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of policy rates below 0.5 percent and unemployment above 5.0 percent creates a liquidity trap dynamic where markets price in prolonged economic stagnation, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 70 percent of cases when both threshold conditions are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate profit margins, triggering negative equity returns over the following month as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of low-GDP observations, invalidating the demand shortfall premise.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising wage bills and consumer income, generating positive short-term equity momentum through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Correlation between payroll changes and 5-day forward returns drops below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, rising retail sales fail to offset eroding real purchasing power, causing anticipated discount rate hikes to dominate earnings growth and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in more than 65 percent of observations when the consumer price index exceeds the specified threshold.
